name: Maskscar Plaza District
identity: Public ceremonial district where masks, banners, dance terraces, and civic braziers keep renewal visible in urban life.
approach: Wide plazas, mosaic routes, and workshop facades show a settlement designed for processional movement.
inside: Mask workshops, banner halls, and rehearsal terraces make ceremony a labor system as well as a public identity.
who_is_out_of_place: those seeking private control of public observance
expected_behavior: treat banners and masks as dynastic civic property
faith: explicitly civic, seasonal, dynastic, and ceremonial rather than temple-centered
seasonal_change: Midyear observance turns the district into the loudest civic space in Psanan.
public_behavior_texture: People who belong here are usually mask makers, banner dyers, dancers, and public conduct is read through expectations such as leave procession routes clear, treat banners and masks as dynastic civic property.
visible_power_signals: Ordinary decisions are visibly shaped by Rebuilding Oath Courts, Great House Citadel Circle.
local_tension_surface: The place feels stable only until pressures such as house rivalry over symbolism, crowd control, heat damage to stored materials begin showing through daily routines.
differentiator: Maskscar Plaza District stands apart because its economy leans on ceremonial production, public event logistics; public faith remains visible through explicitly civic, seasonal, dynastic, and ceremonial rather than temple-centered; outsiders are noticed fastest when they resemble those seeking private control of public observance, thieves of ceremonial goods.
encounter_rhythm: Most first scenes in Maskscar Plaza District begin through ritual plaza district rhythms: arrival, observation, minor correction, and only then deeper access.
